questions.question: Who is being referred to here? ‘But he went out and began to talk freely about it, and to spread the news, so that Jesus could no longer openly enter a town, but was out in the country; and people came to him from every quarter.
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: This passage refers to the leper from Galilee who was healed and then spread the news widely, making it impossible for Jesus to enter towns openly (Luke 5:12-15).

questions.question: ‘An evil and adulterous generation seeks for a sign, but no sign shall be given to it except the sign of Jonah,’ According to St. Mathew, Jesus said this to the
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Jesus made this statement about seeking signs to the Pharisees and Sadducees, the religious leaders who constantly demanded miraculous proof (Matthew 12:38-39).

questions.question: And Jesus said to them, ‘Follow me and I will make you fishers of men. ‘This was addressed to
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Jesus called Simon Peter and Andrew with the words 'Follow me and I will make you fishers of men' while they were casting nets in the sea (Matthew 4:18-19).

questions.question: Hosanna means

options.A: ride on in majesty B.ride on in glory
options.C: ride on to die D.ride on to Calvary

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Hosanna is a Hebrew expression meaning 'save now' or 'save us,' used as an acclamation of praise during Jesus' triumphal entry.

questions.question: One of the reasons why Jesus denounced the Scribes and Pharisees was that they

options.B: did not practice what they preached
options.C: did not preach regularly in the temple
options.D: took great care of the tombs of their ancestors

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Jesus denounced the Scribes and Pharisees because they did not practice what they preached, making them hypocrites.

questions.question: Simon the Cananaean was

options.C: the Pharisee in whose house the woman anointedJesus
options.D: the one who carried Jesus’ cross
options.E: one of the brothers of Jesus.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Simon the Cananaean (also called Simon the Zealot) was one of the twelve apostles chosen by Jesus.

questions.question: The reason why Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Salome went to where Jesus was buried on the third day after the burial was to

options.A: plead with the soldiers who were keeping watch there
options.C: see if the body of Jesus was still there
options.D: see if Jesus had risen from the dead
options.E: weep at the tomb according to the custom of the Jews.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Mary Magdalene and the other women went to the tomb on the third day to anoint Jesus' body according to Jewish burial customs (Mark 16:1).

questions.question: To some who trusted in themselves that they were righteous and despised others, Jesus told the parable of the
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Jesus told the parable of the Pharisee and the tax-collector to those who trusted in their own righteousness and despised others, as recorded in Luke 18:9-14.

questions.question: The main reason why Peter and John were arrested by the officers of the Temple after they had cured the lame man at the gate called beautiful was that they

options.D: abused the elders of the people
options.E: disapproved of worship in the temple

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Peter and John were arrested primarily because they proclaimed Jesus and the resurrection, which was the core issue that disturbed the Temple authorities (Acts 4:2-3).

questions.question: The conversion of Paul was made possible by
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Paul's conversion was directly caused by his encounter with the risen Jesus on the Damascus road, not through any person (Acts 9:3-6).

questions.question: Who is being referred to here? ‘Let his habitation become desolate, and let there be no one to live in it’ and ‘His office let another take.’
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: These quotations from Psalms 69:25 and 109:8 were applied by Peter to Judas Iscariot when explaining the need to replace him among the apostles (Acts 1:20).

questions.question: Which of the following was NOT one of the decisions of the Jerusalem Council communicated to Gentile Christians?

options.A: Abstinence from eating meat offered to idols.
options.C: Avoidance of eating meat with blood in it.
options.D: Abstinence from eating strangled animals’ flesh.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The Jerusalem Council did NOT require circumcision for Gentile Christians; rather, they specifically ruled against burdening Gentiles with circumcision (Acts 15:19-20, 28-29).

questions.question: The proconsul when Paul arrived in Corinth was called
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Gallio was the proconsul of Achaia when Paul arrived in Corinth, as recorded in Acts 18:12.

questions.question: The seven deacons were appointed to

options.A: conduct a mission to the Gentiles
options.D: help in the distribution of food

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The seven deacons were appointed specifically to help distribute food and aid to widows in the early Jerusalem church (Acts 6:1-6).

questions.question: Which ruler said this in the course of Paul’s defense? ‘In a short time you think to make me a Christian!’
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: King Agrippa said this to Paul during his defense, responding to Paul's appeal to convert him to Christianity (Acts 26:28).

questions.question: When Paul arrived in Rome, the people he first addressed were

options.A: the local leaders of the Jews
options.C: The Roman soldiers who were guarding him
options.D: The pagans whom he wanted to convert
options.E: Apollos and his followers who were expecting his arrival.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: When Paul arrived in Rome, he first called together the local leaders of the Jews to explain his situation and preach to them (Acts 28:17).

questions.question: ‘For I am ready not only to be imprisoned but even to die at Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus. ‘Who was this devoted person?

options.E: Barnabas Complete the quotation which relates to Paul’s voyage to Rome Do not be afraid, Paul, you must stand before Caestar, and lo ...

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: This statement about being ready to be imprisoned or die in Jerusalem for Jesus was made by Paul to the disciples at Caesarea (Acts 21:13).

questions.question: ‘This at last is bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh; she shall be called Woman because she was …
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Adam's statement about Eve refers to her being taken out of man's rib, which is the reason for calling her 'Woman' (Isha in Hebrew, derived from Ish meaning man).

questions.question: When God called Abram out of Haran, his first place of call in Canaan was
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: When Abram entered Canaan from Haran, his first stop was at Shechem, where God appeared to him and promised the land to his offspring.

questions.question: ‘This is none other than the house of God, and this is the gate of heaven’, was said by
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Jacob made this statement at Bethel after dreaming of a ladder reaching heaven, recognizing the place as God's house and heaven's gate.

questions.question: The Egyptians enslaved the Israelites and forced them to build
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The Egyptians enslaved the Israelites to build store-cities (Pithom and Raamses) for Pharaoh, as recorded in Exodus 1:11.

questions.question: The Passover was to remind the Israelites of

options.A: God’s execution of judgment on the gods of Egypt
options.B: God’s smiting of all the first born in Egypt
options.C: The seven days of eating unleavened bread
options.D: The eating of roasted lamb while standing.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The Passover commemorated God's sparing of the Israelite firstborns when He smote all the firstborn of Egypt, leading to their deliverance from slavery.

questions.question: The father of Moses was of the tribe of
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Moses' father Amram was of the tribe of Levi, making Moses a Levite by descent.

questions.question: The consecration of the people of Israel took place at the foot of Mount
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The consecration of Israel occurred at Mount Sinai when God gave the law and established the covenant with the people.

questions.question: When did the Israelites say ‘All that the Lord has spoken we will do’?

options.C: When Moses spoke to them before his death
options.D: After the incident of the golden calf
options.E: When God asked them to gather manna from heaven

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The Israelites responded with 'All that the Lord has spoken we will do' at Mount Sinai when God presented the covenant terms to them.

questions.question: What occasion is reflected in this statement? ‘It is not the sound of shouting for victory, or the sound of the cry of defeat, but the sound of singing that I hear.’

options.B: Rejoicing for the water from the rock
options.C: Praising God after receiving the Ten Commandments
options.D: The apostasy of Aaron and the people.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: This statement was made by Moses when he heard the noise from the Israelites worshipping the golden calf, reflecting the apostasy of Aaron and the people.

questions.question: The 5th of the Ten Commandments is a warning

options.A: not to bow down to idols
options.E: to honour one’s father and mother.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The 5th Commandment is 'Honor your father and mother,' which is a warning to respect and obey one's parents.

questions.question: When Moses was instructed to view the Promised Land afar, he

options.B: summoned the elders and informed them about the land
options.D: prayed for a successor to shepherd the people

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Moses was shown the Promised Land from Mount Nebo but was not permitted to enter it; God instructed him to commission Joshua as his successor to lead the people.

questions.question: Hear now, you rebels: shall we bring forth water for you out of this rock? Was spoken by Moses
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Moses spoke these words in the wilderness of Zin when the Israelites complained about lack of water, and he was instructed to speak to the rock to bring forth water.

questions.question: Who gave this advice? ‘Now therefore, fear the Lord and serve him in sincerity and faithfulness; put away the godswhich your fathers served beyond the River and in Egypt, and serve the Lord.’
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Joshua gave this advice to the Israelites at Shechem, calling them to serve the Lord and put away foreign gods after his farewell address.

questions.question: The spies sent out into the land of Canaan were NOT required to find out whether the

options.A: people who dwelt in it were few or many
options.B: land they dwelt in was good or bad
options.C: cities they dwelt in were camps or strongholds
options.D: land was flowing with milk and honey

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The spies were instructed to observe the people, cities, and land quality, but there is no biblical record of them being asked to determine whether the land was forested.

questions.question: To whom was this addressed? ‘Rule over us you and your son and your grandson also, for you have delivered us out of the hand of Midian.
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: This statement was addressed to Gideon after he delivered Israel from the oppression of Midian, and the people offered him rulership which he declined.

questions.question: Who was chosen to lead the Israelites after Moses’ death?
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Joshua was explicitly chosen by God and commissioned by Moses to lead the Israelites after Moses' death.

questions.question: Who asked this question? ‘Which is better for you that all seventy of the sons of Jerubbaal rule over you, or that one rule over you?
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Jotham asked this rhetorical question to the men of Shechem, comparing whether it was better to be ruled by all seventy sons of Jerubbaal or by Abimelech alone.

questions.question: Joshua’ first victory was in
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Joshua's first major victory in Canaan was the conquest of Jericho, where the walls fell down after the Israelites marched around them.

questions.question: At childhood, the word of the Lord came to Samuel because he

options.B: had the qualities of a prophet
options.C: was to announce the punishment for the house of Eli

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: God called Samuel as a child to announce the judgment that would come upon the house of Eli because of the sins of his sons.

questions.question: Sisera was put to death by
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Jael, a woman from the tent of Heber the Kenite, killed Sisera by driving a tent peg through his temple while he slept.

questions.question: ‘Cursed be the man who takes food before evening before I am able to avenge myself on my enemies.’ This was said by Saul during the battle between Israel and the
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Saul made this vow during the battle against the Philistines, as recorded in 1 Samuel 14.

questions.question: ‘But I will be with you and you will smite the Midianites as one man’ was said by God to
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: God spoke this promise to Gideon to encourage him before the battle against the Midianites (Judges 6:16).

questions.question: Who was the man who, in an attempt to prevent a disaster, touched the Ark and died when David was returning it to Jerusalem?
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Uzzah died when he reached out to steady the Ark of the Covenant during its transport to Jerusalem (2 Samuel 6:6-7).

questions.question: The name ‘Samuel’ means

options.C: I have asked him of the Lord
options.E: The future prophet of the Lord.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The name Samuel (Hebrew: Shemuel) means 'I have asked him of the Lord,' reflecting Hannah's prayer for a son (1 Samuel 1:20).

questions.question: The suggestion made by prophet Nathan to Bathsheba to foil Adonijah’s attempt to usurp the throne of David was to

options.B: remind David of his promise that Solomon would succeed him
options.C: have Solomon secretly anointed by Zadok
options.D: plead with Adonijah to abandon the attempt.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Nathan advised Bathsheba to remind King David of his promise that Solomon would be his successor to prevent Adonijah's usurpation (1 Kings 1:13).

questions.question: Samuel’s sons were rejected as judges because

options.B: they were not from the royal family
options.C: they perverted justice and took bribes
options.E: the Israelites wanted to be like other people who had kings

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Samuel's sons Joel and Abijah were rejected because they perverted justice, took bribes, and did not walk in his ways (1 Samuel 8:3).

questions.question: It was wrong for King Solomon to have married foreign women because

options.A: they would entice him to worship idols
options.B: the women of Israel were more beautiful
options.D: a King should not have too many children

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Solomon's foreign wives turned his heart away to worship their gods and idols, which was explicitly forbidden (1 Kings 11:1-4).

questions.question: God took the Kingdom from Saul because he

options.B: did not offer sacrifices to the Lord
options.C: disobeyed the Lord by offering burnt sacrifices himself

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: God rejected Saul because he disobeyed by offering burnt sacrifices himself instead of waiting for Samuel, violating God's command (1 Samuel 13:8-14).

questions.question: How many months were the Israelites on forced labour in Lebanon allowed to spend at home for every month at the labour camp?
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: According to 1 Kings 5:14, the forced laborers in Lebanon worked one month and rested at home for two months in a rotation system.

questions.question: How was Saul killed?

options.B: He took his own sword and fell upon it.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Saul took his own sword and fell upon it to avoid being captured and mistreated by the Philistines (1 Samuel 31:4).

questions.question: Which of the following Kings said ‘Behold your gods, of Israel, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt?
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Jeroboam I made two golden calves and said this statement to the Israelites to prevent them from going to Jerusalem to worship (1 Kings 12:28).

questions.question: The commander of David’s army was
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Joab was the commander-in-chief of David's army throughout his reign (2 Samuel 8:16).

questions.question: King David committed adultery with
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: King David committed adultery with Bathsheba, the wife of Uriah the Hittite (2 Samuel 11:1-5).

questions.question: Which prophet encouraged Jeroboam I to revolt at the initial stage?
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The prophet Ahijah encouraged Jeroboam I to revolt by tearing his garment into twelve pieces as a sign (1 Kings 11:29-39).

questions.question: The city of Samaria was built by
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: King Omri built and established the city of Samaria as the capital of the northern kingdom (1 Kings 16:24).

questions.question: The prophet from Tekoa was
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Amos was the prophet from Tekoa, a town in Judah (Amos 1:1).

questions.question: Which king reigned after Hezekiah?
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Manasseh reigned after his father Hezekiah (2 Kings 20:21, 21:1).

questions.question: Which was NOT a complaint of the people when Nahemiah was rebuilding Jerusalem?

options.A: Let us get grain; that we may eat and keep alive.
options.B: We are mortgaging our fields, our vineyards and our houses.
options.C: We have borrowed money for the King’s tax.
options.D: The strength of the burden-bearers is failing.
options.E: We are forcing our sons and daughters to be slaves.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: While the people complained about grain, mortgaging property, borrowed money for taxes, and enslaving children, there is no biblical record of their complaint about the strength of burden-bearers failing during Nehemiah's rebuilding.

questions.question: Jesus was said to be ‘of Nazareth’ because

options.B: his ancestor David lived in Nazareth
options.C: he was brought up in the city of Nazareth
options.D: he made Nazareth his preaching headquarters.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Jesus was called 'of Nazareth' because he was brought up and raised in the city of Nazareth after his family moved there from Bethlehem (Matthew 2:23, Luke 4:16).

questions.question: Luke gave the most detailed account of Christ’s infancy because he

options.C: Was writing an orderly account of events
options.E: Copied from one of the evangelists.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Luke explicitly states in his prologue that he wrote an orderly account of events (Luke 1:3), which explains his detailed attention to the infancy narratives.

questions.question: ‘You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?’ was part of the Preaching of
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: John the Baptist used the phrase 'brood of vipers' when warning people to flee from the wrath to come, as recorded in Matthew 3:7.

questions.question: Which of these statements is correct?

options.A: The order in which Jesus was tempted Varies in Matthew and Luke but the tests remain the same.
options.B: Matthew follows Luke’s order in which Jesus was tempted.
options.C: The temptation story of Jesus is found in Mark only.
options.D: Only Luke gives us the story of Jesus temptation.
options.E: The story of Jesus’ temptation is found in Luke and John only.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Matthew and Luke both record Jesus' temptation but in different orders - Matthew places the temple temptation last while Luke places the kingdoms temptation last, though the three temptations remain the same.

questions.question: Who of these was NOT one of the twelve?
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Luke was not one of the twelve apostles; he was a companion of Paul and the author of the Gospel of Luke, while Peter, Andrew, James, and John were all among the twelve.

questions.question: Who said ‘I am not worthy to have you come under my roof?
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The Centurion at Capernaum said 'I am not worthy to have you come under my roof' when Jesus offered to heal his servant (Matthew 8:8).

questions.question: ‘The Kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in field’. This parable teaches about the

options.C: urgency of entry into the Kingdom

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The parable of the hidden treasure teaches about the great value and preciousness of the Kingdom of heaven, for which one should be willing to sacrifice everything.

questions.question: The renowned interpreters of the Mosaic Law in the time of Jesus were the
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The Scribes were the renowned interpreters and teachers of the Mosaic Law during Jesus' time, though the Pharisees also upheld the law strictly.

questions.question: ‘This is my beloved son: listen to him’, was said
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: 'This is my beloved son: listen to him' was spoken at the Transfiguration of Jesus when Peter, James, and John witnessed his glorification.

questions.question: ‘The Kingdom of heaven may be compared to a man who sowed good seeds in his field; but while men were sleeping his enemy came and sowed weeds amongst the wheat, and went away.’ The owner of the field was

options.A: the devilB. a son of the Kingdom
options.E: a son of the evil one.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: In the Parable of the Weeds, the owner of the field represents the Son of Man (Jesus), who sows good seeds in his field.

questions.question: When Herod the Tetrarch heard of the fame of Jesus, he thought that He was

options.C: John the Baptist raised from the dead

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: When Herod heard of Jesus' fame, he thought Jesus was John the Baptist raised from the dead, since Herod had previously executed John.

questions.question: ‘Grant us to sit, one at your right hand and one at your, left, in your glory.’ The petitioners were
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: James and John (the sons of Zebedee, also called the 'Sons of Thunder') asked Jesus to grant them seats at his right and left in his glory (Mark 10:37).

questions.question: Today salvation has come to this house, for this is also a son of Abraham. This was said of
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Jesus said this statement to Zacchaeus the tax collector after visiting his house, declaring salvation had come to him as a son of Abraham (Luke 19:9).

questions.question: In the parable of the ten maidens, the five were called foolish they

options.A: were sleeping before the marriage feast
options.D: did not go out of meet the bridegroom
options.E: did not take additional oil with them.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The five foolish maidens were called foolish because they did not take additional oil with them for their lamps while waiting for the bridegroom (Matthew 25:1-13).

questions.question: ‘I find no crime in this man. ‘Who made this statement?
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Pontius Pilate made this statement about Jesus during the trial, finding no crime or fault in him (Luke 23:4).

questions.question: Concerning the events that will precede the end of the world, Jesus asked his followers to pray that it would not happen
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Jesus asked his followers to pray that their flight would not happen in winter, as it would be difficult to escape during that season (Matthew 24:20).

questions.question: Theophillus was

options.B: The one for whom the Act of the Apostle was written
options.C: The centurion converted by Peter in Ceasarea

questions.subject: crk

questions.question: And the angel said to him, ‘Dress yourself and put on your sandal’. The addressee was
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The angel said this to Peter when instructing him to get up and dress himself, leading him out of prison (Acts 12:8).

questions.question: On the road to Gaza, Philip converted and Baptized a
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Philip converted and baptized the Minister (treasurer) of the Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, on the road to Gaza (Acts 8:26-39).

questions.question: After his conversion Paul first went to
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: After his conversion on the Damascus road, Paul (Saul) went to Damascus where he regained his sight and was baptized (Acts 9:1-25).

questions.question: An eloquent Jew from Alexandria, well versed in the Scriptures, was
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Apollos was an eloquent Jew from Alexandria who was well versed in the Scriptures and mighty in his understanding of the way of the Lord (Acts 18:24-25).

questions.question: The ecumenical Council of 49 A.D held in Jerusalem was mainly to discuss the
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The Council of Jerusalem in 49 A.D. was mainly convened to discuss whether gentile Christians should be circumcised and follow Jewish laws (Acts 15).

questions.question: An alter was set up to the Unknown God in the Greek town of
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: An altar was set up to the Unknown God in Athens, which Paul referred to when preaching to the Athenians (Acts 17:22-23).

questions.question: ‘You have appealed to Caesar: To Caesar then you shall go. This statement was made to Paul by
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Festus, the Roman governor of Judea, made this statement to Paul regarding his appeal to Caesar in Acts 25:12.

questions.question: The people of Malta thought that Paul must be a murderer because he

options.A: was bitten by a poisonous snake
options.C: was being taken to Rome as a prisoner
options.D: preached to them about the crucifixion of Jesus
options.E: was going to answer charges before Caesar.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The Maltese people thought Paul was a murderer because he was bitten by a viper and survived, believing divine justice was catching up with him (Acts 28:4).

questions.question: ‘Our sister, be the mother of thousands and ten thousands; and my your descendants posses who was the sister?
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Rebekah was blessed with these words by Isaac's servants, making her the mother of thousands and ten thousands (Genesis 24:60).

questions.question: ‘Cast out this slave woman with her son; for the son of this slave woman shall not be heir with my son Isaac. Who was the slave woman?
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Hagar was the slave woman (Egyptian maidservant of Sarah) whom Abraham was commanded to cast out along with her son Ishmael (Genesis 21:10).

questions.question: ‘… thoms and thistles it shall bring forth to you and you shall eat the plants of field. In the sweat of your face youshall eat bread’.To whom were these words spoken?
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: These words were spoken to Adam as punishment for his disobedience in the Garden of Eden (Genesis 3:18-19).

questions.question: Abraham circumcised his son, Isaac, on the eighth day because.

options.A: It was the custom of his tribe to do so
options.B: God commanded him to do so
options.C: He wanted to distinguish Isaac from Ishmael
options.D: Isaac was the son of promise.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Abraham circumcised Isaac on the eighth day because God specifically commanded him to do so as part of the covenant (Genesis 17:10-12).

questions.question: Who among Joseph’s brothers was bound by Joseph when they went to Egypt to buy grain?
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Simeon was the brother bound by Joseph as a pledge to ensure the other brothers would return with Benjamin (Genesis 42:24).

questions.question: Joshua was buried in the hill-country of
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Joshua was buried in the hill-country of Ephraim, in Timnath-serah, which had been given to him as his inheritance (Joshua 24:30).

questions.question: ‘Take handfuls of ashes from the kiln and let Moses throw them toward heaven I the sight of Pharaoh’.

options.A: the death of all Egyptian flocks and cattle
options.B: the death of the first born and beast
options.C: boils ad sores on man and beast

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The ashes thrown toward heaven by Moses caused boils and sores on man and beast, representing the sixth plague (Exodus 9:8-10).

questions.question: Whoever touches the ‘mountain of God’ shall be put to death by
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Whoever touches the mountain of God (Mount Sinai) shall be put to death by beating or stoning, not by other means (Exodus 19:12-13).

questions.question: ‘Take off the rings gold which are in the ears of your wives, your sons and your daughters and bring them to me’.
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Aaron asked the Israelites for their gold rings to fashion a molten calf as an idol when Moses delayed coming down from Mount Sinai.

questions.question: God commanded that Aaron should speak for Moses to the Israelites, but Moses should be to Aaron as
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: God told Moses that he would be to Aaron as God, meaning Moses would be the authority figure and Aaron would follow his commands.

questions.question: On the first day when the Israelites left part of the bread, it bred worms and became foul, but on the sixth day when they left part of the bread it did not become foul because

options.B: God commanded them to keep it
options.C: Moses wanted to save them the trouble of searching for bread everyday
options.D: by them they have discovered a better way of preserving it.

questions.ai_explanation: Romans 12:6 teaches that believers have gifts that differ according to the grace given to each person by God.

questions.question: According to Romans, as one man’s trespass led to condemnation all, so will on man’s act of righteousness lead to

options.A: acquittal and life for all men
options.B: salvation and new life for all men
options.C: justification and hope for all men
options.D: sanctification and life for all men

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Romans 5:18 states that as one trespass led to condemnation for all men, so one act of righteousness leads to acquittal and life for all men.

questions.question: ‘ And let steadfastness have its full effect …’ What does James say would be the outcome of this effect?
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: James 1:4 declares that steadfastness must have its full effect so that believers may be perfect and complete, lacking nothing.

questions.question: ‘Have this mind among yourselves, which is yours in Christ Jesus…’ This mind in Paul’s statement above means a mind of
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Philippians 2:5 refers to the mind of Christ, which exemplifies humility, sacrifice, and holiness, the attitude Christians should adopt.

questions.question: Peter teaches that servants should be submissive to their masters with all respect, not only to the kind and gentle but also to the
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: 1 Peter 2:18 instructs servants to submit to their masters with respect, not only to the kind and gentle but also to the crooked or wicked.

questions.question: In Romans, Christians are admonished to put on the Lord Jesus Christ and not to
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Romans 13:14 advises believers to put on the Lord Jesus Christ and make no provision for the flesh to gratify its desires.

questions.question: Paul teaches that those who have been baptized into Christ been baptized into His
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Romans 6:3 teaches that those baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into His death, symbolizing union with Him in His death and resurrection.

questions.question: On the third day of their capture of the Ark of God, the Philistines discovered that

options.A: Dagon, their god, had fallen and broken to pieces
options.B: Dagon had disappeared from its place
options.C: The Ark has swallowed Dagon, their god
options.D: The Ark had been standing on Dagon.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: When the Philistines captured the Ark of God, they placed it in Dagon's temple, but on the third day they found Dagon had fallen face down before the Ark and was broken to pieces (1 Samuel 5:3-4).

questions.question: One of the sins of Manasseh for which the LORD send bands of the Chaldeans, Syrians, Moabites and Ammonites to destroy Judah was

options.A: the release of a thousand captives
options.B: his failure to obey God’s prophets
options.C: the arrest of the King of Babylon

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Manasseh's shedding of innocent blood was among his great sins that provoked God to send bands of Chaldeans, Syrians, Moabites and Ammonites against Judah (2 Kings 24:3-4).

questions.question: Elijah decreed that there would be neither rain nor dew for three years because

options.A: Ahab had forsaken the commandment of God by erecting an altar for Baal
options.B: Nebat the son of Rehoboam connived with Ahab to kill him
options.C: he was sure that he would be fed by the ravens
options.D: Jezebel the wife of Ahab killed Obadiah.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Elijah declared a drought because Ahab had forsaken God's commandments and erected an altar for Baal, turning to worship false gods (1 Kings 17:1 and context).

questions.question: God called Jonah and send him to the people of Nineveh to

options.B: pray to Him for their forgiveness

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: God sent Jonah to Nineveh to cry against the city's wickedness, calling them to repentance (Jonah 1:2).

questions.question: Consequent upon the murder of Naboth by Ahab and Jezebel, God declared that

options.A: dogs would lick Ahab’s blood where they had licked Naboth’s
options.B: Ahab’s descendants would never ascend the throne is Israel
options.C: He would require the blood of Naboth from Ahab’s hand
options.D: the sword would not depart from the house of Ahab.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: God declared that dogs would lick Ahab's blood in the same place where they licked Naboth's blood as a judgment for the murder and theft of Naboth's vineyard (1 Kings 21:19).

questions.question: According to Amos, God decided to raise up a nation that would fight and oppress Israel because the people

options.A: were corrupt and enslaved the poor
options.B: claimed to achieve fame by their own strength
options.C: rejected him and sold the righteous for silver

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: According to Amos, God would raise a nation against Israel because they were corrupt, oppressed the poor, and enslaved them (Amos 6:11-14).

questions.question: What did king Josiah do to the altar that was erected by Jeroboam at Bethel?

options.C: He broke its stones into pieces

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: King Josiah broke the altar that Jeroboam erected at Bethel into pieces and burned the Asherah pole as part of his religious reforms (2 Kings 23:15).

questions.question: ‘ “ Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, and before you were born I consecrated you; I appointed you a prophet to the nations.’’ ’ The statements above was made by God during the

options.B: he was very excited about the discovery
options.C: he realized that God was angry with Israel
options.D: no one was able to read the word of the book.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: This quote is from God's call to the prophet Jeremiah, not Isaiah, during his consecration as a prophet to the nations (Jeremiah 1:5).

questions.question: According to Jeremiah, other nations would find glory in God and bless themselves in Him only when Israel

options.A: swears in truth, in justice and in uprightness
options.B: becomes loyal to God and repents of her sins
options.C: returns to God, honours and glorifies Him
options.D: comes to God with prayer and fasting

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: According to Jeremiah, other nations would find glory in God and bless themselves in Him when Israel swears in truth, in justice and in uprightness (Jeremiah 4:2).

questions.question: Hosea proclaimed that Israel should return to God and plead that he should
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Hosea proclaimed that Israel should return to God and plead with Him to take away their iniquity and accept them (Hosea 14:2).

questions.question: The response of Eli after Samuel had told him the Lord’s message as regards his sons’ acts of blasphemy was that

options.A: sacrifice would be offered on their behalf
options.B: he would call them and rebuke them
options.C: God should have mercy on them
options.D: He was the LORD, and should do what pleased Him.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Eli's response to Samuel's message about his sons' blasphemy was acceptance that the LORD would do what pleased Him, demonstrating his submission to God's will (1 Samuel 3:18).

questions.question: Rehab’s reward for hiding the messengers sent by Joshua to spy out the city of Jericho was that

options.A: she and her household were spared
options.B: the city of Jericho was spared for her sake
options.C: she married one of the spies and became a queen
options.D: the gold, bronze and iron in the city were given to her

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Rahab and her entire household were spared from destruction when Jericho fell because of her assistance to Joshua's spies (Joshua 6:22-25).

questions.question: David’s victory over he Amalekites who raided Ziklag was due to

options.A: the fact that David was a mighty man in battle
options.B: his swearing to the Egyptian servant of the Amalekites not to kill or harm him
options.C: the great army that followed him to fight the Amalekites
options.D: his prayer in seeking the approval of God before pursuing them

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: David's victory over the Amalekites was secured after he inquired of the LORD and received His approval to pursue them (1 Samuel 30:8).

questions.question: God did not lead the Israelites by the way of the land of the Philistines although that was near because

options.A: He did not want them to see war and return to Egypt
options.B: The Egyptians would have overtaken them
options.C: They were afraid of the Philistines
options.D: He wanted them to learn through hardship in the wilderness

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: God did not lead Israel through the Philistines' land because He knew they would encounter war and desire to return to Egypt due to fear (Exodus 13:17).

questions.question: One of king Solomon’s unwise policies was the

options.A: incessant fighting of wars against his enemies
options.B: signing of treaties with the kings of the surrounding nations
options.C: use of forced labour to build the house of God
options.D: making of sacrifices in high places. [PAGE 59]

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Solomon's use of forced labour to build the house of God and other structures was considered unwise and eventually led to rebellion under his son Rehoboam (1 Kings 12:4).

questions.question: ‘ “It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him a helper fit for him.’’ ’ After this statement, the first thing God did was to

options.A: form beasts and bird and bring them to the man
options.B: take one of the man’s ribs and close up its place with flesh
options.C: make a rib into a woman and bring her to the man
options.D: cause a deep sleep to fall upon the man.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: After God said He would make a helper for man, the first action was to cause a deep sleep to fall upon Adam before creating woman from his rib (Genesis 2:21).

questions.question: Joseph’s brothers decided not to kill him because

options.C: was their brother and their flesh
options.D: was the first son of his mother

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Joseph's brothers decided not to kill him because he was their brother and their flesh, with Reuben specifically appealing to this kinship (Genesis 37:26-27).

questions.question: King Saul ordered that those who had opposed his ascent to the throne should not be put to death because

options.A: God had wrought deliverance in Israel
options.B: They had fought bravely in battle
options.C: They had been reconciled with him
options.D: God had forgiven their evil intentions

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Saul ordered that those who opposed him not be put to death because God had wrought deliverance in Israel, recognizing God's hand in his ascension (1 Samuel 11:13).

questions.question: After Saul realized that God would have nothing to do with him, he sought the help of
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: After realizing God had abandoned him, Saul sought help from a medium (the witch at Endor) to contact Samuel's spirit (1 Samuel 28:7-8).

questions.question: A promise made by king Saul to anyone that could face and defeat Goliath was

options.A: giving his daughter in marriage to the person
options.B: making the person second in command in Israel
options.C: giving the person part of his land
options.D: making the person the captain of his army.

questions.subject: crk

questions.question: The LORD said to Moses that the people should gather just a day’s portion of bread so that He might test their
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The LORD told Moses the people should gather only a day's portion of manna so He might test their obedience to His instructions (Exodus 16:4).

questions.question: What were the Israelites asked to do before meeting with God on Mount Sinai?

options.A: To be consecrated have their garments washed
options.B: To touch the border of the mountain
options.C: To fast and have their feet washed
options.D: To fast, pray and be consecrated.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Before meeting God on Mount Sinai, the Israelites were commanded to be consecrated and have their garments washed (Exodus 19:10).

questions.question: ‘ “ To you I will give all this authority and their glory; for it has been delivered to me, and I give it to whom I will…”’ This statement was made during the
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: This statement about being given authority and glory was made by Satan during the temptation of Jesus in the wilderness (Luke 4:6).

questions.question: ‘ “ … Just so, I tell you, there is joy before the angels of God over on sinner who repents.’’ ’ Jesus made this statements after telling the parable of the
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Jesus made this statement about joy over a repenting sinner after telling the parable of the lost coin (Luke 15:8-10).

questions.question: According to Luke, the leper healed by Jesus was commanded to

options.A: go and show himself to his friends
options.B: find out from the priest what he should offer
options.D: make an offering for his cleansing.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Jesus commanded the healed leper to go to the priest and make an offering for his cleansing as required by the law (Luke 5:14).

questions.question: After the great confession by Peter, Jesus charged the disciple to

options.A: announce that the kingdom of heaven had c ome
options.B: tell no one that he was the Christ
options.C: go away from the crowd to rest
options.D: beware of the leaven of the Pharisees.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: After Peter's great confession that Jesus is the Christ, Jesus charged the disciples to tell no one that he was the Christ (Matthew 16:20).

questions.question: The Angel of the Lord told Zachariah that the son would

options.A: make the disobedient repent of their sins
options.B: turn may of the sons and daughters of Israel to God
options.C: go before God in the spirit and power of Elijah
options.D: turn the hearts of the children to their father.

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The Angel Gabriel told Zachariah that John the Baptist would go before God in the spirit and power of Elijah (Luke 1:17).

questions.question: When they returned from their mission, the seventy reported that

options.C: they saw Satan fall like lightning
options.D: even demons were subject to them

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The seventy disciples reported to Jesus that even demons were subject to them in his name (Luke 10:17).

questions.question: “Take heart, it is I; have no fear.’’’ This statement was made by Jesus when

options.A: He appeared to two disciples on the way to Emmaus
options.B: He was walking on the sea in the night
options.C: Mary Magdalene saw him at the sepulehre
options.D: The women saw Him after resurrection

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: Jesus said 'Take heart, it is I; have no fear' when he was walking on the sea in the night (Matthew 14:27).

questions.question: “… Blessed is the kingdom of our father David that is coming…” The declaration above was made by the crowd during the
questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The declaration 'Blessed is the kingdom of our father David that is coming' was made by the crowd during Jesus's triumphal entry into Jerusalem (Mark 11:10).

questions.question: After healing the man at the pool of Bathzatha, the Jews sought all the more to kill Jesus because

options.A: they hated the man that was healed
options.B: he did his on the Sabbath
options.C: they were unhappy that he healed the man
options.D: he claimed that God his father was still working

questions.subject: crk
questions.ai_explanation: The Jews sought to kill Jesus because he healed the man at the pool of Bethzatha on the Sabbath, violating their Sabbath laws (John 5:16).
